items.title: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has 'very good links with the IRGC'
items.description: Jean-Emile Jammine is pleased to welcome Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Associate Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) for Defence and International Security Studies in London. The succession process currently unfolding in Iran must be understood within the extraordinary context of wartime decision-making, Dr Hellyer explains. The debate over Mojtaba Khamenei’s appointment is less about dynastic succession than about institutional power inside the Islamic Republic. Hence, his relationship with Iran's powerful IRGC is of utmost importance.

items.title: Iran's new supreme leader's first statement: Takeaways, optics, messaging
items.description: In his first message since he was appointed Iran's new supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei ordered the vital Strait of Hormuz oil shipping lane to remain closed and called on Gulf Arab states to "shut down" US bases. It was a message of defiance and continuity, explains FRANCE 24's Leela Jacinto.

items.title: How Iran’s attacks on Gulf energy sites are shaping decision making in Washington
items.description: Sparking turmoil in global energy markets and undermining Gulf countries’ reputations for economic stability is all part of the Iranian regime’s strategy, according to a Middle East analyst. Speaking to FRANCE 24, Paymon Azmoudeh, senior consultant in the risk and compliance practice at Forward Global, explains that Tehran hopes to pressure Washington to end the war. Iran, he says, is very aware of the political risks for Donald Trump if the global – and US – economy takes a hit, especially with mid-term elections later this year. “When you have Abbas Araghchi, for example, the foreign minister, going on Fox News and NBC […] they’re basically making a mid-term pitch,” he adds.

items.title: Thinking à la française: How French intellectuals shaped the world
items.description: Nerd Alert! This week we focus on France’s contribution to knowledge and ideas. If French philosopher René Descartes famously said, “I think, therefore I am,” then “we think, therefore we are” could be said of the French. Over the years, France has produced a wide array of world-leading thinkers at the forefront of dazzling theories about knowledge, liberty, justice, the human condition – and, of course, the meaning of life. But ask people to name a French intellectual today, and chances are they’ll mention someone long deceased: Camus, Sartre, de Beauvoir, Foucault… It begs the question: where are the intellectuals now?

Novelist Dinaw Mengestu on the battle for freedom of expression
items.description: As he takes on the presidency of PEN America, Dinaw Mengestu explains why defending a plurality of voices is crucial at a time of censorship and revisionism. He also discusses how the organisation is working to rebuild its credibility after criticism over its failure to condemn the war in Gaza in 2023. The award-winning author’s fourth novel, “Someone Like Us”, has just been published in France, and Mengestu tells us about the personal elements he has woven into this story of an Ethiopian-American grappling with his identity. We also discuss the immigrant experience in the United States and why notions of “us” and “them” remain such divisive forces in public debate.

items.title: Two votes, two standards: UN resolutions on Iran war underscores diplomatic dilemmas
items.description: The UN Security Council held two votes on the Middle East war, the first since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran. A Bahrain-sponsored resolution condemning Iran -- but not the US and Israel -- was adopted. A second, Russian-sponsored one, condemning Iran and the US-Israel, was vetoed. FRANCE 24's Leela Jacinto examines the implications of the UN votes.

items.title: Gabon asks IMF for new arrangement in a bid to stabilise finances
items.description: Gabon has formally requested an arrangement with the International Monetary Fund, IMF officials said on Wednesday. The news comes just days after an IMF team concluded a visit to the Central African country.

items.title: News24 | Trump administration spent $11.3bn in first few days of Iran war, may ask for more cash
items.description: Officials from US President Donald Trump’s administration estimated during a congressional briefing this week that the ‌first six days of the war on Iran had cost the United States at least $11.3 billion, a source familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Lebanon: Israeli strikes Beirut in new 'lage-scale' attack
items.description: #Israel launched a new 'large-scale attack' against #Hezbollah targets late on March 11, following rocket fire from the militant group. It struck #Beirut's southern suburbs as well as the capital's city centre, for the second time in a day and the third time since the start of the #war.

items.title: News24 | Saudi Arabia shoots down drones as Iran vows to target US, Israel economic interests
items.description: Saudi Arabia intercepted two drones headed toward the Shaybah oil field, as Iran targets the facility again in its campaign to disrupt global energy markets.

items.title: Sudan assures on fuel supply security amid panic buying
items.description: Oil minister Mutasim Ibrahim told a press conference that Sudan has a 16-day reserve of gasoline, 21 days of diesel, and 17 days of aviation fuel, with more supply on the way

items.title: Germany to adjust heating law to allow fossil fuels
items.description: Fossil fuel prices are rising in response to global conflicts and military actions. Still, Germany is revising its policy for heating private homes to focus less on renewables. Climate activists are up in arms.

items.title: Could Germany adopt AI giant Anthropic?
items.description: After Donald Trump ordered the US government to cancel all contracts with Anthropic, a German politician has said Germany should offer to bring the AI firm to Europe. Is that viable?

items.title: Denis Sassou Nguesso: Congo-Brazzaville's strongman
items.description: One of Africa's longest-serving leaders, Denis Sassou Nguesso has ruled Congo-Brazzaville for decades, keeping a firm grip on power in the former French colony.

items.title: Lucid unveils steering wheel-free robotaxi concept, taking aim at Tesla's Cybercab
Archbishop Gustavo Oscar Carrara: ‘Argentina has 6,000 villas housing five million people’
items.description: Gustavo Oscar Carrara, the Metropolitan Archbishop of La Plata, warns about the defunding of urbanisation works in low-income neighbourhoods and the potential elimination of government department dedicated to socio-urban integration. Leer más

items.title: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
items.description: Biniam Solomon was known for his witty and critical cartoons before the free press was closed.

items.title: DR Congo authorities begin investigations into drone strike
items.description: Authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo said on Thursday that they had begun investigations into the drone attack that targeted the residence of a UN staff member, killing a French aid worker.

items.title: Oil expats desperately needed in Venezuela don't want to go home
items.description: Expats are torn between a desire to rebuild their homeland and preserving the hard-won prosperity they’ve achieved abroad, for themselves and their families. Leer más

items.title: From Sudan to Lebanon: ‘Wherever we go, war follows us’
items.description: In Beirut, refugees from conflict zones like Sudan and Ethiopia have once again been caught up in war — the very thing they hoped to leave behind in their home countries. FRANCE 24's reporter spoke to families sheltering at a Jesuit church in the Lebanese capital, who have lost everything for a second time after fleeing Israeli and US air strikes on Lebanon.

items.title: Iran sets three conditions for ending war
items.description: During nuclear talks ahead of the war, Iran had consistently argued that as a signatory of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), nuclear enrichment is ‘a legitimate right’. This was also the first time a senior Iranian leader called for reparations as a condition to end the war

items.title: Cruise ship too fast, too wide before hitting reef, initial report finds
items.description: An early safety report into the grounding of luxury Australian cruise ship Coral Adventurer focuses on its speed and a wide turn in confined waters before it swerved into a reef.

items.title: Youth-backed RSP win signals Nepal’s new political era
items.description: The election result signals Nepal’s democratic evolution and India needs to play a supportive role

items.title: The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis
items.description: The latest on the Iran war and the growing oil crisis across the Middle East from Dubai, Iraq, Lebanon and Israel.

items.title: Children among victims in Pakistan’s shelling in Afghanistan: Taliban
items.description: Cross-border clashes between the two countries intensified on February 26, two days before US, Israel attacked Iran.

items.title: Thousands of white South Africans repatriate as US safety fears grow
items.description: Thousands of white South Africans are returning home after years abroad, reversing a long-standing trend of emigration. Many are reclaiming citizenship through a government portal launched after the repeal of a 1995 law that had stripped citizenship from some expatriates.

items.title: Argentina: the safest country in the world
items.description: With the world in chaos, Milei returned to the basics and relied on the same kind of things that got him elected in the first place. Leer más

items.title: Survey: 69% of young adults in Argentina have little or no interest in politics
items.description: Research among voting-age students finds most teenagers disengaged from politics; Democracy still widely valued despite scepticism about the country’s direction. Leer más

items.title: Cape leopard's return sparks hope
items.description: In South Africa's Western Cape mountains, the return of a top predator is giving conservationists hope after 170 years. But with fewer than a thousand Cape leopards left in the wild, the species remains dangerously vulnerable.

items.title: Trump says Iran shouldn't come to World Cup for 'own life and safety'
items.description: US President Donald Trump warned Iran's football team Thursday their "life and safety" would be at risk if they took part in the upcoming World Cup in North America.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Israel army says Hezbollah fired 200 rockets in 'biggest barrage' of war
items.description: Israel's military said Thursday that Hezbollah had fired around 200 rockets at Israel the night before, in what it described as the Lebanese armed group's "biggest barrage" since the war began. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Israel pounds Lebanon: Grieving border village calls for peace
items.description: In the south Lebanon border village of Qlayaa, hundreds mourn parish priest Father Pierre al-Rai two days after he was killed by Israeli shelling while inspecting the site of an earlier attack. Amid the funeral procession, residents implore the army for protection from the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah, but pledge to stay no matter what.

items.title: Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations
items.description: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warning for residents in southern Lebanon.

items.title: News24 | Spain to expand healthcare access to undocumented migrants
items.description: Spain’s left-wing government announced on Tuesday it will expand free healthcare to undocumented migrants, bucking a European trend toward stricter immigration policies.

items.title: Prosecutor in Argentina pushes AMIA bombing case toward trial in absentia
items.description: Thirty years on, prosecutor seeks trial in AMIA bombing; Ten suspects and newly identified Iranian official targeted in renewed push for justice. Leer más

items.title: Germany: 7-year-old flashes €5,000 cash at Osnabrück school
items.description: The youngster found a cash-filled envelope at home that his parents had withdrawn to buy a car, but not before he took it to class for an extra-curricular "if I were a rich man" lesson with his friends.

items.title: Chinese man arrested in Kenya with 2,000 live queen ants at Nairobi airport
items.description: A Chinese national has been arrested at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport in Nairobi after authorities found more than 2,000 live queen garden ants hidden in his luggage, Kenyan officials said.

items.title: Balendra Shah’s RSP party of wins majority: Nepal Election Commission
items.description: In direct elections, RSP won 125 of the 165, and secured another 57 in PR votes, leaving them only two seats short of securing a powerful two-thirds majority

items.title: Israeli billionaire's firm settles DR Congo graft probe for $30 million
items.description: A former holding company of Israeli billionaire Dan Gertler is paying $30 billion to settle a Dutch corruption investigation over mining deals in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.title: News24 | Nigeria’s new corridor opens doors for jihadist groups
items.description: Nigeria’s northwest and central border regions have become a hub for Sahelian militants, raising concerns about a widening insurgency corridor that could destabilise the region.

items.title: Iran war: How long before Gulf nations stop pumping oil?
items.description: The oil price spiked near $120 after strikes on Iran's energy sites and the shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. With tankers stuck and Gulf oil facilities hit, producers are running short on storage to maintain production.

items.title: Germany news: Lufthansa pilots stage another strike
items.description: A two-day strike by Lufthansa pilots is expected to lead to many cancellations. The German chancellor is visiting Norway, a key energy supplier to his country. DW has more on events in and concerning Germany.

items.title: Wheels within wheels
items.description: Milei’s insults only served to lend importance to the fading force of Kirchnerism, perhaps a deliberate strategy to preserve an electoral opponent virtually guaranteeing him victory. Leer más

items.title: Atlanta bets on its film future amid industry slowdown
items.description: Atlanta, long known as the Hollywood of the South, saw a boom in recent years thanks to blockbuster superhero films like Superman and hit TV series. But movie production across Georgia has slowed, with much of it moving overseas.

items.title: U.S. has ‘practically nothing left to target’ in Iran, says Trump: report
items.description: Trump has given repeated mixed messages about the timing and aims of the war but on Monday (March 9, 2026) he also said the ending could come “soon.”

items.title: Trump says U.S. benefits from high oil prices; Iran, Israel continue to exchange fire
items.description: Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran’s National Security Council, responded to Mr. Trump, saying if Iran’s electricity grid is targeted, “the whole region will go dark”

items.title: News24 | 16 ships hit in Strait of Hormuz, too risky to provide escorts, says US Navy
items.description: Explosive-laden Iranian boats appear to have attacked two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters, setting them ablaze and killing one crew member, after projectiles struck four vessels in Gulf waters, said port, maritime security and risk firms.

items.title: Milei reaffirms alliance with Trump at ‘Shield of the Americas’ summit
items.description: Milei in attendance at international meeting in Florida; Trump offers Latin America’s leaders US missile strikes to hit drug cartels, calls for tougher action on organised crime, hints at Cuba action. Leer más

items.title: Merz says Germany won't join EU return to nuclear energy
items.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is in favor of a proposal to build new nuclear power plants in the EU. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says it's impossible.

items.title: Nigeria: Dangote refinery supports oil distribution amid Iran war
items.description: In order to avoid shortages as the war in the Middle East rages on, Nigeria can rely upon billionaire Aliko Dangote’s refinery in Lagos, which is capable of processing some 650,000 barrels of crude oil per day.

items.title: Iran: Funeral procession held in Tehran for military officials
items.description: Thousands gathered in the Iranian capital for a funeral procession in memory of several top military leaders, the country's state media reported on Wednesday.

items.title: Chinese national arrested over attempt to smuggle 2,000 queen ants from Kenya
items.description: The suspect had packed some ants in test tubes while others were concealed in tissue paper rolls, prosecutor says.

items.title: Meta AI’s ‘Vibes’ feature floods platform with sexual videos of children and explicit Bollywood deepfakes
items.description: The short AI videos in the app feed, which Meta calls ‘Vibes,’ often feature children’s faces on the bodies of adult women, as well as sexual scenes featuring morphed celebrity lookalikes

items.title: Honda expects FY2025/26 loss of up to $4.3 billion from review of EV strategy
Watch: Two fuel tankers burning after suspected Iran attack in Gulf
